official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Open-Label Study Comparing the Bedaquiline-Pretomanid-Linezolid (BPaL) Regimen to WHO-Recommended Longer MDR-TB Regimen in Adults with Multidrug-Resistant Pulmonary Tuberculosis
interventions: BPaL regimen: Bedaquiline 400mg daily x 2 weeks then 200mg 3x/week, Pretomanid 200mg daily, Linezolid 1200mg daily (reduced to 600mg after 16 weeks) for 26 weeks
brief_summary: This Phase 3 study compares the efficacy and safety of the shortened BPaL regimen (26 weeks) versus the WHO-recommended longer regimen for multidrug-resistant pulmonary tuberculosis. The primary endpoint is favorable outcome (culture conversion and no relapse) at 73 weeks post-randomization.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Adults aged 18-65 years

2. Pulmonary tuberculosis confirmed by sputum culture or GeneXpert

3. Documented multidrug-resistant TB (resistance to at least isoniazid and rifampicin)

4. No prior treatment with bedaquiline, pretomanid, or delamanid

5. Prior MDR-TB treatment of <= 2 weeks duration allowed

6. Sputum smear positive or culture positive at screening

7. Body weight >= 35 kg

8. Adequate hepatic function (ALT/AST <= 3x ULN, total bilirubin <= 2x ULN)

9. Serum albumin >= 2.8 g/dL

10. QTcF < 500 ms on screening ECG

11. HIV-positive patients eligible if on stable ART with CD4 > 100 cells/μL

12. Written informed consent

13. Willingness to be hospitalized for initial treatment phase if required
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Extensively drug-resistant TB (XDR-TB) with fluoroquinolone resistance

2. Known allergy to any component of the BPaL regimen

3. Peripheral neuropathy CTCAE Grade >= 2

4. Optic neuritis or significant visual impairment

5. Hemoglobin < 8 g/dL or platelet count < 50,000/μL

6. Pregnant or breastfeeding

7. Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inducers

8. Active extrapulmonary TB involving the CNS

9. Karnofsky Performance Status < 50

inclusion_criteria.text: Pulmonary tuberculosis confirmed by sputum culture or GeneXpert
Documented multidrug-resistant TB (resistance to at least isoniazid and rifampicin)
No prior treatment with bedaquiline, pretomanid, or delamanid
Prior MDR-TB treatment of <= 2 weeks duration allowed
Sputum smear positive or culture positive at screening
Adequate hepatic function (ALT/AST <= 3x ULN, total bilirubin <= 2x ULN)
HIV-positive patients eligible if on stable ART with CD4 > 100 cells/μL
Willingness to be hospitalized for initial treatment phase if required

exclusion_criteria.text: Extensively drug-resistant TB (XDR-TB) with fluoroquinolone resistance
Known allergy to any component of the BPaL regimen
Optic neuritis or significant visual impairment
Concurrent use of strong CYP3A4 inducers
Active extrapulmonary TB involving the CNS
